description_long: This is a traditional windmill with a tall, open lattice metal tower and a circular rotor featuring multiple flat, rust-colored blades. The windmill's rotor is mounted at the top of the tower, with a large tail vane extending horizontally to help orient the blades into the wind. The structure includes a ladder running up one side of the tower, providing access for maintenance. The metal surfaces show a weathered, matte finish, suggesting exposure to the elements. The overall design is utilitarian, typical of windmills used for water pumping or small-scale power generation in rural or urban fringe settings.
description: A tall, lattice metal windmill with a circular, multi-bladed rotor and a large tail vane, featuring a weathered, rust-colored finish.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the full circular rotor with all blades visible and the ladder on the tower.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the rotor, the horizontal tail vane, and the thin profile of the blades.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the rotor, the tail vane, and the ladder structure on the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the tail vane and rotor profile visible, emphasizing the lattice structure of the tower.
